description: Update the normative redesign docs and the `DMS-1103` / `DMS-930` story text to define the corrected semantic-identity contract: non-reference persisted multi-item scopes compile semantic identity from `arrayUniquenessConstraints`, while reference-backed persisted multi-item scopes compile it from the scope-local `documentPathsMapping.referenceJsonPaths` order for exactly one qualifying reference binding. Touch `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/11-stable-collection-row-identity.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/01-reference-and-constraints.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/flattening-reconstitution.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/profiles.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/summary.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/data-model.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/transactions-and-concurrency.md`, and `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/key-unification.md`. Acceptance: all of those docs say the same thing about the two allowed schema sources, the strict fail-fast behavior when neither source yields a non-empty identity, the rule that reference-derived collection identity binds to the reference FK column in `referenceJsonPaths` order rather than to propagated identity columns, and the rule that API-semantic collection UNIQUE constraints derive from the compiled semantic identity rather than from `arrayUniquenessConstraints` alone.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "arrayUniquenessConstraints|referenceJsonPaths|semantic identity|reference-backed|non-reference|compiled semantic identity" reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/11-stable-collection-row-identity.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/01-reference-and-constraints.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/flattening-reconstitution.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/profiles.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/summary.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/data-model.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/transactions-and-concurrency.md reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/key-unification.md` and confirm the wording is consistent across all touched files.

Review the updated sections and confirm none of those docs still describe `arrayUniquenessConstraints` as the only semantic-identity source for reference-backed persisted multi-item scopes or as the sole source of collection semantic-identity UNIQUE derivation.

description: Update downstream epic sibling stories and branch tracking artifacts so they consume compiled semantic identity rather than hard-coding `arrayUniquenessConstraints` as the only upstream source. Touch `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/07-index-and-trigger-inventory.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/02-ddl-emission/07-stable-collection-row-identity-ddl.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03-persist-and-batch.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/05-write-error-mapping.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md`, and `tasks.json`. Acceptance: sibling stories refer to the upstream compiled semantic identity or the appropriate schema source rather than to `arrayUniquenessConstraints` alone, the index-inventory story tracks collection semantic-identity UNIQUEs as compiled-identity-driven inventory, `tasks.json` no longer frames semantic identity as AUC-only, and the repo root no longer carries a `PROBLEMS.md` blocker telling us to edit authoritative ds-5.2 inputs.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "arrayUniquenessConstraints|compiled semantic identity|appropriate schema source|referenceJsonPaths|semantic-identity UNIQUE|request-validation failures keyed" reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/07-index-and-trigger-inventory.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/02-ddl-emission/07-stable-collection-row-identity-ddl.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03-persist-and-batch.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/05-write-error-mapping.md reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md tasks.json` and confirm the updated language matches the corrected contract.
Run: `test ! -f PROBLEMS.md` and confirm there is no repo-root blocker file telling us to edit authoritative ds-5.2 inputs.

description: Refactor the relational-model pass pipeline so semantic-identity compilation is a distinct staged concern ahead of fail-fast validation and downstream constraint/index consumers. Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/RelationalModelSetPasses.cs` and the current `ArrayUniquenessConstraintPass`-centered implementation so AUC-driven identity compilation for non-reference scopes is preserved, but no longer remains the only path that can populate `DbTableModel.IdentityMetadata.SemanticIdentityBindings`. Acceptance: there is a deterministic semantic-identity compilation stage before validation and before stable-collection constraint derivation, and downstream consumers read compiled identity metadata rather than re-deriving scope semantics from raw `arrayUniquenessConstraints` inputs.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit coverage that exercises the ordered pass list and proves semantic identity is compiled before fail-fast validation and constraint derivation.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend semantic-identity compilation so persisted multi-item scopes with empty semantic identity after AUC processing can derive it from exactly one scope-local `DocumentReferenceBinding`. Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ArrayUniquenessConstraintPass.cs` or the extracted semantic-identity compiler to: find exactly one qualifying scope-local reference binding on the table, emit `CollectionSemanticIdentityBinding` entries in `referenceJsonPaths` order using scope-relative `ReferenceJsonPath` values, and bind every emitted member to the reference `FkColumn`. Acceptance: reference-backed collection/common-type/extension-collection scopes compile non-empty semantic identity without AUC when there is exactly one qualifying scope-local reference binding; non-reference scopes still depend on AUC; the implementation never falls back to `Ordinal`, `CollectionItemId`, or parent-only locators.
steps-to-verify: Add or update relational-model unit tests that cover top-level and nested reference-backed collection scopes with no `arrayUniquenessConstraints` entry.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add an explicit fail-fast validation pass after semantic-identity compilation and before downstream consumers in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/RelationalModelSetPasses.cs`. Acceptance: supported persisted multi-item scopes fail deterministically with distinct diagnostics for (1) missing `arrayUniquenessConstraints` on a non-reference-backed scope, (2) ambiguous reference-derived identity when zero or multiple scope-local reference bindings qualify for a reference-backed scope that still has empty identity, and (3) any remaining unsupported persisted multi-item scope that still cannot produce a non-empty semantic identity after both sources are considered.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests that assert the exact diagnostic categories and messages for each new failure mode.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update stable collection constraint derivation so semantic-identity uniqueness is emitted from the compiled identity metadata rather than from `arrayUniquenessConstraints` resolution alone. Touch `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/StableCollectionConstraintPass.cs` and any supporting helpers so semantic-identity UNIQUE constraints are derived from `(ImmediateParentScopeLocatorColumns..., SemanticIdentityBindings[*].ColumnName)` with deterministic de-duplication. Acceptance: reference-backed scopes produce semantic-identity uniqueness on `(ParentScopeLocator..., <Reference>_DocumentId)` after de-duplication, non-reference scopes still use their compiled AUC-backed columns, and sibling-order plus composite parent/root consistency constraints remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Update `ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests` to assert semantic-identity UNIQUE constraints are derived from compiled identity metadata for both AUC-backed and reference-backed scopes.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests.cs` with focused positive cases for reference-derived collection identity. Acceptance: tests cover at least one top-level and one nested persisted multi-item scope that omit AUC, assert the `SemanticIdentityBindings` relative-path order matches `referenceJsonPaths` order, assert all compiled identity bindings map to the reference FK column, and assert the resulting semantic-identity UNIQUE constraint de-duplicates repeated FK-column members correctly.
steps-to-verify: Add the new positive cases beside the existing semantic-identity coverage in `ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests.cs`.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add negative coverage for the new fail-fast rules beside `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/SchemaInputValidationTests.cs` or in a dedicated pass-level validation test file. Acceptance: tests distinguish the missing-non-reference-AUC case, the ambiguous-reference-derived case, and the unsupported-scope case; they assert the intended diagnostics without modifying authoritative ds-5.2 inputs or collapsing the separate Core validation paths for reference-array uniqueness and `arrayUniquenessConstraints`.
steps-to-verify: Add or update validation-focused tests to assert each diagnostic path independently.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update only the hand-authored positive fixtures that are genuinely out of contract under the corrected rule, while leaving authoritative upstream fixtures unchanged. Focus on non-reference positive fixtures that really do need AUC, such as the `PersonSite` / `PersonAddress` cases called out by the blocker analysis. Acceptance: hand-authored non-reference persisted multi-item scopes compile with valid AUC metadata, reference-backed authoritative scopes compile from `documentPathsMapping.referenceJsonPaths`, and `src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2/inputs/ds-5.2-api-schema-authoritative.json` remains untouched.
steps-to-verify: Update the relevant hand-authored fixture inputs under `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/Fixtures` and any mirrored plan-fixture directories that rely on those hand-authored schemas.
Run: `git diff -- src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2/inputs/ds-5.2-api-schema-authoritative.json` and confirm there is no diff for the authoritative ds-5.2 input file.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Refresh expected manifests and goldens to reflect reference-derived semantic identity, without changing authoritative inputs. Update the relational-model expected artifacts so authoritative ds-5.2 outputs now show non-empty semantic identity and semantic-identity uniqueness for scopes like `Ed-Fi:Assessment` `$.programs[*]` / `edfi.AssessmentProgram`. Acceptance: authoritative expected manifests and any focused-fixture goldens align with the corrected compiler behavior, and the expected artifacts prove the fix was to generalize DMS semantic-identity compilation rather than to redefine upstream schema metadata.
steps-to-verify: Refresh the expected JSON artifacts under `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/Fixtures/authoritative` and any focused fixture directories touched by the new positive or negative coverage.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`
